title: Getting started with baselinr
text: Every quasi-experimental impact study education answer question anyone looks outcomes: treatment comparison groups similar enough baseline? Works Clearinghouse (WWC) sets de facto standard education research: covariate standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) 0.05 less satisfies baseline equivalence ; 0.05 0.25, equivalence holds covariate statistically adjusted impact model; 0.25, covariate establish equivalence. baselinr computes effect sizes categories baseline table something assemble hand every report.

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: default, every numeric column treatment indicator treated covariate. Pass covariates = control set explicitly.
code: study <- data.frame(   treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6) )  baseline_equivalence(study, treatment = "treat") #>   covariate n_treatment n_comparison mean_treatment mean_comparison #> 1   pretest           3            3              6               5 #>   sd_treatment sd_comparison hedges_g  wwc_category #> 1            1             1      0.8 not_satisfied

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: baseline_equivalence() built two exported helpers can also call directly.
code: # Standardized mean difference (Hedges' g) for one covariate hedges_g(study$pretest, study$treat) #> [1] 0.8  # Classify any effect size(s) into the WWC categories wwc_classify(c(0.03, 0.12, 0.80)) #> [1] "satisfied"                 "satisfied_with_adjustment" #> [3] "not_satisfied"

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: Version 0.1.0 handles continuous covariates. Binary covariates (via WWC Cox index), formatted report output, Love plot roadmap — see NEWS.md.

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: MIT © baselinr authors

title: Summary
text: quasi-experimental randomized impact evaluations education programs, analysts must demonstrate treatment comparison groups similar baseline claim program effects credible. U.S. education research, Works Clearinghouse (WWC) sets de facto standard judgment: baseline covariate summarized standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) classified one three categories determine whether covariate must handled impact model [@wwc2022]. baselinr small R package automates specific, repetitive task. Given data frame, treatment indicator, set continuous covariates, returns report-ready table containing group’s sample size, mean, standard deviation; standardized mean difference WWC small-sample correction; corresponding WWC baseline-equivalence category.

title: Summary
text: Assessing baseline equivalence WWC conventions routine error-prone hand-work: analysts repeatedly compute Hedges’ g small-sample correction factor, apply 0.05 0.25 thresholds, assemble table every report, often spreadsheet software ad hoc scripts. General-purpose R tools covariate balance, cobalt [@greifer2023], compute wide range balance statistics matching weighting workflows encode specific WWC reporting categories education evaluation reports required state. baselinr deliberately occupies narrow gap tools: thin, education-specific reporting layer produces exact baseline-equivalence categorization expected WWC-aligned reporting, modeling assumptions minimal interface. intended users applied education researchers, evaluators, graduate students need reproducible, transparent baseline table rather general causal-inference framework.

title: Summary
text: hedges_g() computes standardized mean difference treatment comparison group single continuous covariate, using pooled within-group standard deviation small-sample correction factor ω=1−3/(4N−9)\omega = 1 - 3 / (4N - 9), NN combined sample size [@hedges1981]. wwc_classify() maps standardized mean differences three WWC categories: satisfied (|g|≤0.05|g| \le 0.05), satisfied_with_adjustment (0.05<|g|≤0.250.05 < |g| \le 0.25), not_satisfied (|g|>0.25|g| > 0.25) [@wwc2022]. baseline_equivalence() combines one-row-per-covariate table group sizes, means, standard deviations, Hedges’ g, WWC category. package depends base R [@rcoreteam], covered unit tests, ships worked example vignette.

title: Summary
code: library(baselinr)  study <- data.frame(   treat   = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6) )  baseline_equivalence(study, treatment = "treat") #>   covariate n_treatment n_comparison mean_treatment mean_comparison #> 1   pretest           3            3              6               5 #>   sd_treatment sd_comparison hedges_g  wwc_category #> 1            1             1      0.8 not_satisfied

title: Summary
text: Version 0.1.0 handles continuous covariates. Binary covariates (via WWC Cox index), formatted report output, Love plot standardized mean differences across covariates planned. baselinr reports baseline-equivalence statistics; select covariates, fit impact models, make adequacy determinations user’s behalf.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: data data frame. treatment String naming column data identifies group membership. Must exactly two unique non-missing values (see hedges_g() treatment group determined). covariates Character vector column names evaluate. Defaults numeric columns data treatment.

text: data frame one row per covariate columns covariate, n_treatment, n_comparison, mean_treatment, mean_comparison, sd_treatment, sd_comparison, hedges_g, wwc_category.

what: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: Computes standardized mean difference (Hedges' g) treatment comparison group single numeric covariate, using pooled within-group standard deviation small-sample correction factor used Works Clearinghouse (WWC).

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: x Numeric vector covariate values. treatment Vector length x identifying group membership. Must exactly two unique non-missing values. larger value (e.g. 1, TRUE, second sorted level) treated treatment group; comparison group. na.rm Logical; drop rows x treatment NA. Default TRUE.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: single numeric value: Hedges' g. Positive treatment group mean exceeds comparison group mean.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: correction factor \(\omega = 1 - 3 / (4N - 9)\), \(N = n_{treatment} + n_{comparison}\).

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g

what: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: Maps standardized mean differences three Works Clearinghouse baseline-equivalence categories. Sign ignored; classification uses absolute value effect size.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: es Numeric vector standardized mean differences (e.g. values returned hedges_g()).

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: character vector length es: "satisfied" |es| <= 0.05 (adjustment needed), "satisfied_with_adjustment" 0.05 < |es| <= 0.25 (equivalence holds covariate adjusted impact model), "not_satisfied" |es| > 0.25. NA inputs return NA.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
baselinr 0.1.0
text: Initial scaffold. hedges_g(): standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) treatment comparison group, WWC small-sample correction factor. wwc_classify(): classify standardized mean differences three WWC baseline-equivalence categories. baseline_equivalence(): build report-ready baseline equivalence table continuous covariates.

title: baselinr 0.1.0
text: Binary covariates via WWC Cox index. Formatted output reports (gt / flextable). Love plot standardized mean differences across covariates.
